What We Do

Our Programs

For over 88 years, the Kiwanis Club of Stuart has delivered programs that serve children and families throughout Martin County. Every initiative is driven by the dedication of our members and the generosity of our community.

Literacy

Kiwanis Reads

Kiwanis Reads is our flagship literacy initiative, dedicated to putting books in the hands of every child in Martin County. We believe that reading is the foundation for lifelong success, and our programs make that foundation accessible to all.

Through Little Free Libraries installed throughout Kiwanis Park, children and families can take and share books year-round. Our Celebrity Readers in the Park events bring community leaders, educators, and local figures to read aloud to children in an engaging outdoor setting.

We also distribute free books at Pancakes & Reading events and during our monthly Super Saturday gatherings at the park.

We welcome gently read book donations. To arrange a donation or pickup, contact Judy Snyder at Judith.Snyder47@gmail.com.

Support Kiwanis Reads Donate Books
Community Giving

Saturday with Santa

For more than 60 years, Saturday with Santa has been one of our most treasured traditions. Each year, we partner with local schools and social services to identify children in need and provide them with clothing, shoes, and Christmas gifts.

The day is about much more than gifts. Children enjoy a free lunch, holiday crafts, free books, fairy hair, and face painting. A donation of $75 helps sponsor a child for the event.

Volunteers help with shopping, wrapping, and making the day special. If you'd like to sponsor a child or volunteer your time, we'd love to have you involved.

Sponsor a Child -- $75 Volunteer With Us
Monthly Events

Super Saturday

Super Saturday is a monthly community event held at Kiwanis Park at Woodlawn on the 3rd Saturday of each month, September through May. Families come together for activities focused primarily on STEM and reading in a safe and welcoming outdoor environment.

Each Super Saturday features hands-on STEM activities, reading programs, volunteer readers, and community resource tables for children of all ages. It's a true reflection of what Kiwanis is about: neighbors serving neighbors.

See Upcoming Dates Volunteer
Signature Event

Pancakes & Reading in the Park

Held the first Saturday in March in honor of Dr. Seuss' birthday, Pancakes & Reading in the Park is one of our most beloved annual events. The event is free and open to everyone. Families enjoy an All-You-Can-Eat breakfast feast of "Green Eggs and Ham," pancakes, scrambled eggs, sausage, ham, fresh fruit, coffee, tea, and orange juice.

Dr. Seuss characters and special guest readers in costume provide great fun and photo opportunities. Community partners set up booths throughout the park, including the Martin County Library, Friends of the Library, the Children's Museum, Helping People Succeed's Healthy Families, Children's Services Council HIPPY Program, DAR with flag kits, the Humane Society's Rescue Readers, the Master Gardeners, and local police handing out badges to kids. When available, the Martin County Sheriff's Department brings K-9 Patches, their therapy dog.

We also host a huge Art in the Park chalk contest where kids compete for prizes by drawing a literary character from their favorite book. Our K-Kids paint and hide rocks for a unique twist on an Easter egg hunt called "Kiwanis Rocks!"

View Event Calendar Get Involved
Scholarships

Robert Whitty Agricultural Scholarship

Named in honor of longtime member Robert Whitty, this scholarship supports Martin County students pursuing careers in agriculture. The award recognizes academic achievement, community involvement, and dedication to the agricultural field.

Agriculture is a cornerstone of Martin County's heritage and economy. This scholarship ensures the next generation of agricultural leaders has the support they need to succeed.

For information on eligibility requirements and how to apply, please contact us directly.

Contact Us to Apply
